input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The `Light` and `Dark` values are applied automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:**

```csharp
// For Color properties specifically:
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

// For any property (generic version):
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

---

**Key points:**

- Reacts to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`AppTheme.Light` / `AppTheme.Dark`) and updates live when the user toggles the system theme.
- Works on any `BindableProperty` — colors, images, strings, etc.
- For app-wide consistency, define themed values in a `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of repeating hex codes:

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- In your page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBg}" />
```

- To detect or override the theme in code:

```csharp
// Read current theme
AppTheme current = Application.Current.RequestedTheme;

// Force a specific theme (overrides system)
Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change task on an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
